result:
T2 complete. Summary of what the orchestration produced:

## Deliverables (all in the working directory)
- **`script.R`** — self-contained; Okabe-Ito palette + `theme_amce` declared at top, `set.seed(1234)` before estimation.
- **`figures/amce-dotwhisker.png`** — 2850×2700 px @ 300 dpi, no in-plot title; dot-and-whisker grouped by attribute, levels ordered within attribute, references fixed at zero (black), estimates blue with 95% CIs, dashed line at 0.
- **`report.md`** — ~180-word results paragraph plus the figure reference with its caption underneath.
- `estimates.csv` — the plotted numbers, for inspection.

## Estimation choices (lead-level calls)
- `projoint(out, .estimand="amce", .structure="profile_level")` with `.qoi=NULL` estimates all seven attributes at once; `.auto_cluster=TRUE` gives **respondent-clustered SEs** natively — no deviation from projoint's estimator.
- Reported the **IRR-corrected** AMCE as primary, since the repeated-flipped outcome was explicitly wired into `reshape_projoint` and is consumed only to compute that correction. (Uncorrected estimates are directionally identical.)

## Headline findings
Largest effects: **+20% crime −25.1 pp**, **75-min commute −23.7 pp**, **40%-income housing cost −19.8 pp**; positive movers are small-town (+15.8), suburban-mixed (+14.6), rural (+13.5), and top schools (+11.6). Racial composition and presidential vote are indistinguishable from zero.

## Orchestration accounting
- **1 of 3 delegations used** (fast-worker wrote + ran the script).
- **1 revision cycle used** (my direct edit fixing clipped vertical facet-strip labels → horizontal, fully legible).
- No Codex/Opus parallel path — the estimand call was verifiable, not "high-blast-radius AND hard-to-verify."
